About InterDigital InterDigital is a global research and development company focused primarily on wireless, video, artificial intelligence (“AI”), and related technologies. We design and develop foundational technologies that enable connected, immersive experiences in a broad range of communications and entertainment products and services. We license our innovations worldwide to companies providing such products and services, including makers of wireless communications devices, consumer electronics, IoT devices, cars and other motor vehicles, and providers of cloud-based services such as video streaming. job_description: About InterDigitalInterDigital is a global research and development company focused primarily on wireless, video, artificial intelligence (“AI”), and related technologies. We design and develop foundational technologies that enable connected, immersive experiences in a broad range of communications and entertainment products and services. We license our innovations worldwide to companies providing such products and services, including makers of wireless communications devices, consumer electronics, IoT devices, cars and other motor vehicles, and providers of cloud-based services such as video streaming. As a leader in wireless technology, our engineers have designed and developed a wide range of innovations that are used in wireless products and networks, from the earliest digital cellular systems to 5G and today’s most advanced Wi-Fi technologies. Founded in 1972, InterDigital is listed on Nasdaq.InterDigital is a registered trademark of InterDigital, Inc. For more information, visit: www.interdigital.com.Job SummaryThe InterDigital AI Lab is seeking motivated Ph.D. and Master students for internships in Deep Learning for Image and Video Processing.The Lab’s research focuses on novel applications of AI and machine learning for video and wireless applications. Unlike traditional roles that involve tinkering with obscure parts of ads or social networks, you’ll have the opportunity to develop proof-of-concepts, prototypes, and research ideas that integrate machine learning and AI technologies with a wide range of video and wireless technologies. Your work will have the chance to be published at leading conferences.Our recent work focuses on distributed, real-time computer vision for edge-cloud systems. We reduce bandwidth usage by compressing intermediate feature maps using a practical mix of learned methods and standard video codecs (e.g., H.264). We mitigate network latency with Dedelayed, a delay-aware split inference technique recently submitted to ICLR. We also created the leading open-source library for deep learning-based image and video compression (github.com/InterDigitalInc/CompressAI), and an open-source platform used by MPEG to benchmark video coding for distributed vision models (github.com/InterDigitalInc/CompressAI-Vision).Come intern with us if you are interested in one of the following research areas:Data compression:Deep learning-based image and video compression.Deep learning-based image and video restoration.Compression for multiple computer vision tasks.Compression of intermediate feature maps for split inference.Compression of tokens and embeddings in ViTs and VLMs.Compression enhanced by language embeddings as side information.Differentiable proxy/surrogate of video codecs; variable-rate control; error resilience.Real-time distributed inference:Distributed real-time video inference resilient to wireless latency, jitter, and bandwidth limits.Vision tasks: semantic segmentation, depth estimation, object detection, future-frame prediction, super-resolution.Motion modelling and prediction for latency mitigation in dynamic scenes.Inference that adapts to fluctuating network, compute, and power constraints.Low-power mobile device models assisted by powerful cloud models in real-time.Applications: autonomous driving, delivery drones, robotics.Efficient model inference.And:Visually realistic Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs).Unique or novel ideas. Qualifications:Ph.D. student in Computer Science/Engineering preferred, Electrical Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, or relevant discipline.Strong background in at least one of computer vision, image processing, or video compression.Proficient in PyTorch (preferred), TensorFlow, or JAX.Internship Benefits:We promote long-lasting collaborations with our interns.Publish in leading conferences including CVPR, ICLR, ICCV, ICASSP, ICIP, DCC, PCS.Opportunity to collaborate with leading researchers in their fields.InterDigital leads the way in Video and Wireless Technologies:Laser focused on research with more than 300 engineers and researchers.We invent solutions for efficient wireless networks, better video delivery, and richer multimedia experiences – more than $1B invested in R&D efforts since 2000.We designed and developed a wide range of technologies used in 4G and 5G wireless, and in many video standards (MPEG, H.265, H.266, …).80% of consumer electronics manufacturers integrate our technology.Internship: Full Time (Resident) onsiteLocation: Los Altos, CAInterDigital is an equal employment opportunity employer. job_title: Data Analyst Intern - Part time - Spring Semester
job_description: Relay is transforming the physical world into a searchable, actionable data asset. We’ve moved beyond "Big Radio's" analog past to build the Relay SOAR™ Platform, which treats voice and movement as the new keyboards of the enterprise. By capturing the "Human Signal," we enable frontline teams in hospitality, manufacturing, and healthcare to move from reactive chaos to proactive control. Ranked among the fastest-growing tech companies in North America, we are looking for the next generation of "Relayers" to help us digitize the final frontier of the workforce. Join us in Raleigh and let’s get LIT. We are looking for a motivated and ambitious Data Analyst Intern to join our Analytics team. Our mission is to collect raw data from a growing number of sources and deliver timely, impactful insights to every department—from Engineering and Operations to Sales and Marketing. This internship is designed for active contribution. You will tackle real business challenges and work on high-impact projects alongside our Analytics team. You will gain hands-on experience leveraging a SQL-based data warehouse, contributing to high-throughput data pipelines, and developing visualizations that directly influence business decisions. High-performing interns may be considered for full-time roles after graduation, depending on business needs. What You Will Do Analyze: Use SQL to extract, clean, and reshape raw data to answer diverse questions from stakeholders. Build & Maintain: Design, develop, and maintain business intelligence dashboards and reports. Collaborate: Work with stakeholders across the organization to understand their data challenges and negotiate effective solutions. Develop: Participate in the team’s software development process, gaining real-world Agile experience and getting code into production. Document: Write clear documentation for new and existing events, tables, and code to ensure knowledge sharing. Required Skills SQL Proficiency: Strong ability to use SQL for data extraction, cleaning, and reshaping. Data Visualization: Strong understanding of visualization techniques to make complex data easily understood. Communication: Excellent ability to communicate clear, concise insights to both technical and non-technical audiences. Technical Curiosity: A creative drive to ask questions and deeply understand the data and the business needs behind it. Scripting: Proficiency in Python or R for data cleaning and scripting. Pragmatism: Good time management skills; you understand that a fast, maintainable solution is often better than a slow, complex one. Preferred Qualifications Experience with a dashboarding tool (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Metabase, etc.). Exposure to general programming and software engineering principles (e.g., version control, modular code, testing). Familiarity with Linux, command line interfaces (CLI), and cloud computing services. Previous experience with SaaS company operations or metrics. Education & Eligibility Actively enrolled and pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Data Science, or similar). Target Graduation Date: May 2026, December 2026, or Spring 2027. Must be local to the Raleigh, NC area and able to work onsite at our headquarters part of the time. Why Start Your Career at Relay? Work at the Edge of Innovation: We just secured our 100th patent. You’ll work with technologies spanning AI-driven noise suppression, real-time language translation, and advanced IoT connectivity. Massive Scale: Be part of a platform that processes over 1 billion data points every week. Our tech isn't just "cool"—it’s essential infrastructure for the global economy. LIT Culture (Learning, Impact, Teamwork): We pride ourselves on an environment where early-career professionals have a seat at the table and see their work impact global brands immediately. Hyper-Growth Trajectory: With 461% growth and new C-suite leaders who have led successful IPOs, you are joining a rocket ship at the perfect time. Best-in-Class Perks: Based in our high-tech Raleigh headquarters, you’ll enjoy 100% company-paid benefits, a 401(k) match, and a hybrid work model that balances flexibility with high-energy collaboration. Note: This is a paid internship position with flexible hours. You can expect to work hybrid hours, combining ~10-15 hours per week onsite at our Raleigh office/headquarters and remotely. If you're passionate about technology and excited to join a company that's shaping the future of work, we encourage you to apply by February 13th!

Teamwork makes the stream work.

Roku is changing how the world watches TV

Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.

From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.

About the team

The Client Strategy team within Ad Sales Marketing brings together Roku’s products and content to create customized marketing solutions for priority advertising partners. We work closely with teams across the company - including Sales, Measurement, Design, Client Services and Product - to develop strategic programs that drive revenue, and bring them to life through clear, compelling storytelling.

We’re focused on building marketing and ad strategy that grows Roku’s advertising business.

About the role

This summer internship places you within Roku’s Ad Sales Marketing team where you’ll gain hands-on experience in media, streaming, and technology. You’ll own a project from start to finish, collaborate with team members to create, review, and streamline sales marketing collateral, manage internal resources, help coordinate cross-functional projects, and participate in meetings, brainstorms, and workshops.

This role requires someone who is organized, leads with a solutions-oriented mindset, and excels in communication skills as they navigate a fast-paced environment.

For New York Only - The estimated hourly rate for this position is between $20 and $50 per hour. Compensation packages are based on factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to skill set, certifications, and specific geographical location. This role is eligible for health insurance, life insurance, disability benefits, parental leave, and wellness benefits.

What you’ll be doing

  • Owning a project that drives value to the team and offers meaningful experience in ad sales marketing
  • Partnering with team members to create, organize and refine sales marketing materials
  • Collaborating with and supporting cross functional teams to support the development of materials that showcase Roku’s products and content to advertisers
  • Attending meetings and brainstorms focused on ad sales client proposals and new product/content initiatives
  • Assisting in identifying workflows that can be streamlined to be more efficient among cross-functional teams

We’re excited if you have

  • Enrolled in a degree program in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field
  • Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite e.g., PowerPoint
  • Excellent problem-solving, communication and organizational skills
  • Interest in streaming, advertising and tech
  • A collaborative sensibility
  • A proactive approach, with a passion for continuous learning

Our Hybrid Work Approach

Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy.

Benefits

Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Our employees can take time off work for vacation and other personal reasons to balance their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter.

Accommodations

Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com.

The Roku Culture

Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV.

We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002.

To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.

If you’re looking for a meaningful career, you’ll find it here at Webster. Founded in 1935, our focus has always been to put people first--doing whatever we can to help individuals, families, businesses and our colleagues achieve their financial goals. As a leading commercial bank, we remain passionate about serving our clients and supporting our communities. Integrity, Collaboration, Accountability, Agility, Respect, Excellence are Webster’s values, these set us apart as a bank and as an employer.

Come join our team where you can expand your career potential, benefit from our robust development opportunities, and enjoy meaningful work!

If you're looking for an exciting opportunity to gain real-world work experience, Webster Bank's internship program delivers! High performing students entering their first or second year of law school will have the opportunity to work side-by-side with Webster colleagues to gain valuable experience, knowledge, and skills. This internship program is designed to create a pipeline of candidates who may become Webster’s next generation of leaders.

As an intern at Webster Bank, you will experience an unforgettable summer of learning, networking, and fun, while gaining valuable experience that directly complements classroom studies. Every internship opportunity is structured to provide you with a meaningful experience, broad exposure to our corporate functions, and opportunities for mentorship and networking. Webster's internship will be demanding & rewarding and prepare you for your career ahead.

The role will provide an opportunity to perform meaningful legal work on a variety of issues in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment. You will have the opportunity to work with attorneys across the Webster Legal Department supporting and advising the array of bank business lines, enabling you to gain a stronger understanding of the legal profession in the banking industry.

Summer Intern Program duration: June 1 – August 7, 2026.

Location: Stamford, CT

Job Responsibilities:

1. Perform substantive legal research, writing, drafting and/or project-based work in the following areas:

  • Corporate Secretary/Governance/Shareholder Services
  • Bank Regulatory
  • Corporate Securities Filings
  • Vendor contracts
  • IP, Privacy & Cyber
  • Risk/Legal Operations
  • Litigation
  • Residential & Consumer Lending
  • Commercial Lending & Payments
  • Employment

2. Attend internal meetings with departments across the bank and interface with management, other legal staff, and Webster colleagues.

With supervision by a senior attorney, may be given responsibility for:

  • Generating first draft analyses of developing issues or responses to inquiries.
  • Developing written presentations to Webster colleagues.
  • Drafting Legal Department procedures or process documents.
  • Reviewing existing contracts for material information and input into contract management system.
  • Developing reporting from legal billing software solution.

3. Work on other tasks as appropriate to support the Webster legal team where needed.

Education, Skills, and Experience Requirements for Interns:

  • Current first or second year Law School student
  • Resourceful, enthusiastic, and proactive
  • Highly organized
  • Demonstrated strong attention to detail
  • Experience creating and delivering presentations
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Self-motivated with a strong work ethic
  • Ability to learn quickly and solve problems
  • Strong analytical skills
  • Collaborative and inclusive team player
  • Proficient in Word, Excel, PowerPoint
  • Legally eligible to work in the U.S.

This is a Hybrid role with a 3 day a week in office requirement

The estimated salary range for this position is $21.00-$23.00 per hour. Actual salary may vary up or down depending on job-related factors which may include knowledge, skills, experience, and location. In addition, this position is eligible for incentive compensation.

<p><b>Company Overview: </b></p><p>At Labcorp, we believe in the power of science to change lives. We are nearly 70,000 people across 100 countries who are harnessing science for human good. Our work combines unparalleled diagnostic laboratories, drug development capabilities and commercial innovations. Together, we fuel scientific breakthroughs and deliver more than 160 million medical test results that help guide treatment decisions, accelerate patient care and change lives.</p><p>Join us in our pursuit of answers<b>. </b></p><p></p><p><b>Labcorp 2026 Global Internship Program Exciting Internship Opportunity – End User Computing!</b></p><p></p><p>Join Labcorp’s 2026 Global Internship Program and take the first step toward a meaningful career in healthcare innovation! We’re looking for motivated students to be part of a dynamic 12-week paid internship where you’ll work on impactful projects and gain real-world experience, all while supporting our mission to improve health and improve lives.</p><p><b> </b></p><p><b>Internship 2026 Dates: May 18, 2026 – August 7, 2026 </b></p><p></p><p><b>About the Program:</b></p><p>As a Labcorp intern, you’ll be immersed in hands-on work that complements your academic background. Job Overview:

Summer 2026 Intern – Sales Operations Analyst – Frisco, TX

As a Summer 2026 Intern – Sales Operations Analyst in Frisco, TX at Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), you will develop critical skills needed to pursue a career in analytics, strategy, shopper insights, or sales at our industry-leading company. You will work across our analytics, planning, sales, marketing, and strategy teams leveraging data & business storytelling to solve real-world market problems or opportunities.

As a Sales Operations Analyst intern, you will be considered to be invited back to participate in our Early Career Leadership Development Program, which lasts up to 1 year and begins once you obtain a Bachelor’s degree.

There are several tracks that can be pursued within this program, including: Insights, Sales Analytics, Category Management, Shopper Insights/Behaviors, National Retail Sales, Revenue Growth Management, or Channel.

Shift/Schedule:

  • The KDP 2026 Summer Internship Program will run from June 1 – August 7, 2026
  • Full-time; 40 hours per week
  • Monday-Friday
  • 8:00am until 5:00pm
  • Hybrid

As a Sales Operations Analyst Intern you will:

  • Build relationships across teams
  • Create selling stories
  • Provide consumer and shopping insights

Elements of the KDP Intern Program include:

  • Engaging and partnering on innovative projects to gain experience in a challenging, cross functional team environment
  • Participating in meet & greets and lunch & learns with KDP executives and other organization leaders
  • Receiving professional development training such as networking, professional skills development and presenting
  • Partnering with a mentor to enhance your knowledge of other parts of the business and build your network
  • Completing a project from start to finish and presenting it and your takeaways to your team, department vice president and other KDP stakeholders
Total Rewards:
  • $28.00/hour
  • Paid bi-weekly
  • $2,500 Sign-on Bonus, paid within 30 days of employment
Requirements:
  • Candidates must be currently enrolled as full-time undergraduate students at an accredited college or university, in their junior year, with an expected graduation date in Fall 2026 or Spring 2027. Eligible majors include Sales, Analytics, Marketing, Communications, or other related fields.
  • Available to work 40 hours per week (M-F, 8am-5pm)
  • Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail with a demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects, prioritize requests, and meet deadlines
  • Strong communication skills including excellent listening, written, and verbal abilities
  • Ability to work cross-functionally, be independently driven, and a self-starter
  • Strong skills in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
  • Consumer packaged goods or beverage experience a plus.
  • Ability to demonstrate strong analytical and project management skills
  • Ability to multi-task in a cross functional environment

Please note: This position is limited to persons with permanent right to work in the United States.

job_description: <h3>The Position</h3><p><u><b>2026 Summer Intern - Regev Lab - Molecular Design</b></u></p><p></p><p><b><b>Department Summary</b></b></p><p></p><p><span>The Regev Lab in Genentech Research and Early Development is seeking an exceptional graduate student intern with a demonstrated research track record in developing computational models to represent and design biology at the molecular and genomic level, and the ability to independently execute on innovative ideas. </span></p><p></p><p><span>This internship position is located in</span> <b>South San Francisco, on-site. </b></p><p></p><p><b><b>The Opportunity</b></b></p><p></p><p><span>Programmable molecular design depends on learning models that can navigate exponentially large sequence spaces, which current experimental screens can only partially sample. job_description: <strong>Job Requirements </strong><br><p><span style="color: black;">Position Summary</span></p><p> </p><p>High school students on the path to a medical career may apply to enter Spartanburg Regional Health Care System’s Work-Based Learning Summer Internship. These students will work alongside professionals in the healthcare environment to learn foundational skills that can transfer to various healthcare fields. Work-based Learning (WBL) combines classroom-based education with on-the-job training experiences to help students prepare for or explore their work-site objectives. Students selected into this program will have the opportunity to practice critical thinking, problem solving, and communication skills while working alongside highly skilled medical professionals. This experience must be planned and supervised by the school and employer so that it contributes to the student’s education and employability. Orientation into this role will be required.</p><p> </p><p><span style="color: black;">Minimum Requirements </span></p><p> </p><p><strong>Education</strong></p><ul><li>Current Junior or Senior enrolled in a Spartanburg, Cherokee, or Union County High School at time of application</li></ul><p><strong>Experience</strong></p><ul><li>N/A</li></ul><p><strong>License/Registration/Certifications</strong></p><ul><li>N/A</li></ul><p> </p><p><span style="color: black;">Preferred Requirements</span></p><p> </p><p><strong>Preferred Education</strong>      </p><ul><li>N/A</li></ul><p><strong>Preferred Experience</strong>     </p><ul><li>N/A</li></ul><p><strong>Preferred License/Registration/Certifications     </strong></p><ul><li>American Heart BLS Provider Course</li></ul><p><span style="color: black;">Core Job Responsibilities</span></p><p> </p><ul><li>Follows assigned department’s guidelines, roles, and responsibilities</li><li>Identifies areas for improvement and seeks ways to work on self-defined weaknesses</li><li>Remains committed to unit, department, and organization goals</li><li>Adheres to proper chain of command</li><li>Demonstrate exceptional customer service and professionalism in all interactions  </li><li>Assist staff to maintain cleanliness of environment </li><li>Stock and/or organize supplies and equipment  </li><li>Run errands, answer unit telephone, answer and respond to call lights </li><li>Follows Infection Control Procedures </li><li>Other duties as assigned by the department</li></ul><br><br>

<h1><b><b><b>Job Description Summary</b></b></b></h1>Join GE HealthCare as the HR Talent Program Intern and play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our recruitment and hiring processes. <br><br>In this role, you’ll help build talent pipelines and support our national Service Apprentice Program. You’ll work closely with leaders on tasks like reviewing resumes, scheduling interviews, and keeping data organized. You’ll also assist with sending exit surveys and updating trackers to monitor current apprentices and those exiting the program.<br><br>Beyond day-to-day tasks, you’ll have the chance to work on projects such as creating an external partnership tracker or developing training sessions for apprentices to support their ongoing skill development.<p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><h1><b>Job Description</b></h1><p><b>Responsibilities</b></p><ul><li><p>Assist with recruiting and hiring activities, including posting jobs in Workday, reviewing resumes, scheduling interviews, and monitoring pre-employment steps.</p></li><li><p>Keep internal trackers up to date to ensure accurate data for workforce planning and program management.</p></li><li><p>Support coordination of training sessions, monthly calls, and other program events.</p></li><li><p>Assist with sending exit surveys and collecting feedback to improve programs.</p></li><li><p>Help maintain external partnership information and support outreach to schools for local talent pipelines.</p></li><li><p>Collaborate with leaders on program initiatives.</p></li><li><p>Contribute to projects such as creating a partnership tracker or developing training sessions for apprentices.</p></li></ul><p></p><p><b>Required Qualifications</b></p><div><ul><li><p>Currently enrolled in an Associate or Bachelor’s degree program.</p></li><li><p>Strong written and verbal communication skills.</p></li><li><p>Good organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks.</p></li><li><p>Proficiency in Microsoft Word and PowerPoint; basic Excel skills for charts and data tables.</p></li><li><p>Comfortable using internet tools and web applications for research and communication.</p></li><li><p><span>Legal authorization to work in the U.S. is required. We will not sponsor individuals for employment visas, now or in the future, for this job.</span></p></li></ul></div><div><p></p><p><b>Desired Characteristics</b></p><ul><li><p>Previous experience in Human Resources: recruitment, learning & development, or program management.</p></li><li><p>Detail-oriented with a process-focused mindset.</p></li><li><p>Familiarity with Workday or other HR systems.</p></li><li><p>Familiarity with Smartsheet.</p></li></ul><p></p><p>For U.S. based positions only, the pay range for this position is $15 - $20 an hour. It is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the pay range and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. The specific compensation offered to a candidate may be influenced by a variety of factors including skills, qualifications, experience and location. In addition, this position may also be eligible to earn performance-based incentive compensation, which may include cash bonus(es) and/or long term incentives (LTI). GE HealthCare offers a competitive benefits package, including not but limited to medical, dental, vision, paid time off, a 401(k) plan with employee and company contribution opportunities, life, disability, and accident insurance, and tuition reimbursement.​</p><p></p><p>We expect all employees to live and breathe our behaviors: to act with humility and build trust; lead with transparency; deliver with focus, and drive ownership – always with unyielding integrity.</p><p></p><p>Our total rewards are designed to unlock your ambition by giving you the boost and flexibility you need to turn your ideas into world-changing realities. Our salary and benefits are everything you’d expect from an organization with global strength and scale, and you’ll be surrounded by career opportunities in a culture that fosters care, collaboration and support.</p></div><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p>We will not sponsor individuals for employment visas, now or in the future, for this job opening.

Any offer of employment is conditioned upon the successful completion of a drug screen (as applicable). </span></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:left">While GE HealthCare does not currently require U.S. employees to be vaccinated against COVID-19, some GE HealthCare customers have vaccination mandates that may apply to certain GE HealthCare employees.</p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><div><div><div><div><div><div><div><p style="text-align:left"><b>Relocation Assistance Provided: </b><span>No</span></p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><p style="text-align:inherit"></p><p style="text-align:inherit"></p>

Role and Responsibilities

Position Summary

This internship focuses on optimizing profitability through the development and implementation of effective promotional strategies for Samsung's TV, Monitor, and Soundbar products. The intern will work closely with cross-functional teams to analyze historical promotional campaigns, design innovative strategies, and monitor their impact on revenue and profitability. This role offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in business management, market research, and financial modeling while contributing to the success of Samsung's product categories.

Key Responsibilities

Promotion Analysis:
• Evaluate historical promotional campaigns to identify what has worked best in terms of revenue growth and profitability.
• Assess the impact of promotions on attach rates and customer engagement.

Strategy Development:
• Design and refine promotional strategies tailored to TV, Monitor, and Soundbar products.
• Propose innovative promotional models, such as bundling or subscription-based offers, to increase attach rates.

Financial Modeling:
• Build financial models to assess the potential impact of proposed promotional strategies on revenue and profitability.
• Evaluate the scalability of these strategies across different markets.

Implementation & Monitoring:
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to execute the developed promotional strategies.
• Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to track success and make data-driven adjustments as needed.

General Responsibilities

Market Research and Analysis:
• Conduct in-depth market research to identify emerging trends, customer preferences, and competitive dynamics.
• Analyze existing revenue streams and promotional strategies to pinpoint opportunities for enhancement.

Reporting and Recommendations:
• Prepare detailed reports on the progress and outcomes of the implemented strategies.
• Offer actionable recommendations for continuous improvement and long-term sustainability.

Qualifications

Education:
• Currently pursuing a Master's Degree.
• Preferred academic fields of study: Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics
• Final-year graduate students are strongly preferred.

Technical Skills:
• Strong analytical skills with proficiency in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, Tableau).
• Experience in market research and strategic planning.
• Knowledge of financial modeling and business metrics.
• Excellent communication and presentation skills to effectively convey ideas and findings.

Skills:
• Experience in e-commerce, digital business development, or site management is a plus.
• Technical understanding of e-commerce platforms and integrations.
• Ability to define and report functional requirements for digital features.
• Adaptable and collaborative in a dynamic digital environment.

This internship is based in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, and offers a unique opportunity to contribute to Samsung's product categories while gaining valuable experience in category business management, market research, and strategic planning. The intern will have the chance to work on impactful projects, collaborate with experienced professionals, and develop skills that are highly relevant in the field of business management.

Skills and Qualifications

The hourly rate for this role is $49. Actual pay will be determined considering factors such as relevant skills and experience, and comparison to other employees in the role.

Are you looking for an exciting job where you can put your skills and talents to work at a company you can feel proud to be a part of? Do you want a workplace that will challenge you and offer you opportunities to learn and grow? A position at Xcel Energy could be just what you’re looking for.

At Xcel Energy, our employees are the driving force behind our success. So we make sure that, here, you can be your best. Doing work that makes a difference for neighbors and communities. Working with a team you can count on to push you. Expanding skills, staying ready for change, and capturing opportunities to grow. All with the support, rewards and recognition you need to thrive – during your internship and beyond.

Project Description

Artificial Intelligence is transforming how work gets done—and Xcel Energy is focused on making sure that transformation delivers real, measurable business value.

We’re looking for an AI Enablement Intern to join a high‑impact team working at the intersection of AI technology, business problem‑solving, and organizational change. This is a cutting‑edge role for someone excited by AI who wants to help shape how it is applied across a large enterprise—not as experimentation, but as a practical driver of productivity and better ways of working.

In this role, you’ll operate much like a business‑facing AI solution architect. You’ll partner with teams across the company to understand how their work actually gets done, identify broken or inefficient processes, and help determine where AI—such as Microsoft Copilot or AI agents—can meaningfully improve outcomes. From there, you’ll help turn those ideas into reality by supporting the thoughtful implementation, adoption, and scaling of AI‑enabled solutions.

At the same time, you’ll contribute to the middle‑out engine that makes AI adoption stick: building community, learning from real wins, and spreading what works so teams can learn from one another. Technology alone doesn’t transform organizations—the combination of the right solutions and the right enablement does. That’s the work this team leads.

This position is for a June 1, 2026 start. The role may have the possibility to extend beyond the internship based on performance and business needs.

What You’ll Work On

This role intentionally blends two dimensions of our AI Enablement strategy:

Top‑Down: AI Solutioning & Business Value

  • Partner with business teams in a consultative manner to understand workflows, pain points, and opportunities for improvement.
  • Help identify where AI is appropriate—and where it isn’t—by mapping real business problems to practical AI solutions.
  • Contribute to the design of AI‑enabled solutions that improve broken or inefficient processes, with a strong focus on value over novelty.
  • Support implementation efforts, recognizing that successful AI adoption requires more than technology—it requires clear communication, change management, and smart rollout approaches.
  • Help assess and communicate impact, including how AI solutions are improving productivity, quality, or the employee experience.

Middle‑Out: Community, Learning & Scale

  • Contribute to building the AI community at Xcel Energy by supporting initiatives like the Copilot Flight Plan, Copilot Champions, and Hero Stories that highlight real success.
  • Help capture and share wins, lessons learned, and best practices so effective AI usage spreads across teams.
  • Support engagement strategies that inspire curiosity, confidence, and responsible use of AI tools.
  • Enable teams to learn from one another by helping package insights, stories, and examples in ways that are easy to reuse and adapt.

You’ll work as part of the AI Enablement team, collaborating internally while also engaging directly with business partners across the organization.

Minimum Requirements

  • Currently enrolled in a bachelor’s or master’s program (as of Fall 2026) in a relevant field (e.g., Business, Information Systems, Engineering, Analytics, Communications, Organizational Development, or similar).
  • Strong curiosity about AI and how it can reshape work—not just from a technology perspective, but from a business and human perspective.
  • Ability to engage with stakeholders, ask good questions, and translate ambiguous problems into clear next steps.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable explaining ideas to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
  • Comfort operating in ambiguity and learning quickly in a fast‑evolving space.
  • Must be a local resident in CO or MN.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience working on cross‑functional projects involving technology, process improvement, consulting, or product strategy.
  • Familiarity with modern AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, automation tools, analytics platforms), or strong interest in learning them quickly.
  • Experience with storytelling, presentations, or content creation that helps spread ideas and best practices.
  • Exposure to change management, adoption strategies, or community‑building efforts.
  • Basic data literacy—comfortable interpreting simple trends or signals to support decision‑making.

Visa sponsorship is not offered for this position.

Company Summary

EchoStar builds solutions that help families and communities stay connected. We’ll launch your career and empower you to change lives.

Our brands include Boost Mobile, DISH TV, Gen Mobile, Hughes and Sling TV. We serve millions of customers with offerings ranging from satellite to streaming services and global to personal networking solutions.

Department Summary

At EchoStar, hard work is rewarded with limitless opportunities. We’re looking at more than your education and previous work experience, we want individuals who are bright, energetic and ambitious. We believe the critical thinking skills you learn in college can be applied across a variety of business functions, regardless of your major. The opportunities at EchoStar will provide you with a potential fast track for career advancement.

Our internship program is designed to give you comprehensive experience in the fast-paced world of wireless and technology industries. With hands-on field experience and business projects, you will get an in-depth look at how our business works and where you fit into it. Help us build our brand while we help you build yours.

Our interns partner with teams across EchoStar working to drive our latest game-changing innovation. Our internship is a 10 week, in-person experience that is paid hourly with eligible overtime. Interns are given a business project(s) to complete over the summer and present their results to leadership and their peers. EchoStar provides each intern with a manager to support them with the execution of their project.

All opportunities are located in Denver, CO unless otherwise stated.

Job Duties and Responsibilities

EchoStar has an exciting opportunity for a Software Engineering Intern to support the Cloud Contact Center team to enhance the monitoring and reporting capabilities of the existing AWS Connect environment. The role will involve developing automated data collection and visualization tools to track system performance, call metrics, and customer interaction trends.

Key Responsibilities:
• Use AWS services such as CloudWatch, QuickSight, and Lambda, to create or improve dashboards and alert systems to monitor key metrics (call volumes, IVR paths, agent utilization, and error trends)
• Integrate a light AI component using Amazon Comprehend to analyze customer sentiment or identify common phrases from call transcripts
• Provide hands-on AWS development experience while helping DISH improve visibility and proactive management of its contact center environment
Skills, Experience and Requirements

Education and Experience:
• GPA 3.3 or above
• Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program, in a related field of study
• Must have 60 credit hours completed by May 2026

Skills and Qualifications:
• Computer Science courses
• Basic proficiency in AWS Services, particularly Amazon Connect, Lambda, CloudFormation, and DynamoDB
• Knowledge of web development technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and RESTful API design
• Experience with or interest in cloud-based contact center technologies and computer telephony integration (CTI)
• Familiarity with SQL or NoSQL databases such as Amazon RDS or DynamoDB
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to troubleshoot issues in real time
• Excellent time management, organization, and communication skills.
• Ability to learn quickly, adapt to change, and work effectively in a fast-paced environment

Visa sponsorship not available for this role

Salary Ranges

Compensation: $18.00/Hour - $24.00/Hour

DESCRIPTION

Do you want to solve real customer problems through innovative technology? Do you enjoy working on scalable services in a collaborative team environment? Do you want to see your code directly impact millions of customers worldwide?

At Amazon, we hire the best minds in technology to innovate and build on behalf of our customers. Customer obsession is part of our company DNA, which has made us one of the world's most beloved brands.

Our Software Development Engineers (SDEs) use modern technology to solve complex problems while seeing their work's impact first-hand. The challenges SDEs solve at Amazon are meaningful and influence millions of customers, sellers, and products globally. We seek individuals passionate about creating new products, features, and services while managing ambiguity in an environment where development cycles are measured in weeks, not years.

At Amazon, we believe in ownership at every level. As an SDE-I, you'll own the entire lifecycle of your code - from design through deployment and ongoing operations. This ownership mindset, combined with our commitment to operational excellence, ensures we deliver the highest quality solutions for our customers.

We're looking for curious minds who think big and want to define tomorrow's technology. At Amazon, you'll grow into the high-impact engineer you know you can be, supported by a culture of learning and mentorship. Every day brings exciting new challenges and opportunities for personal growth.

Amazon internships across all seasons are full-time positions, and intern/co-ops should expect to work in office, Monday-Friday, up to 40 hours per week typically between 8am-5pm. Specific team norms around working hours will be communicated by your manager. Interns should not have conflicts such as classes or other employment during the Amazon work-day. Applicants should have a minimum of one quarter/semester/trimester remaining in their studies after their internship concludes.

Please note that by applying to this role you would be considered for Software Development Engineer summer intern and spring/fall co-op roles on various Robotics teams. You will be able to provide your preference of location and start date during the application process but, we cannot guarantee that we can meet your selection based on several factors including but not limited to the availability and business needs of this role. Finalization on the location and start dates available will be provided to you at the time of job offer.

Key job responsibilities
• Collaborate and communicate effectively with experienced cross-disciplinary Amazonians to design, build, and operate innovative products and services that delight our customers, while participating in technical discussions to drive solutions forward.
• Design and develop scalable solutions using cloud-native architectures and microservices in a large distributed computing environment.
• Participate in code reviews and contribute to technical documentation.
• Build and maintain resilient distributed systems that are scalable, fault-tolerant, and cost-effective.
• Leverage and contribute to the development of GenAI and AI-powered tools to enhance development productivity while staying current with emerging technologies.
• Write clean, maintainable code following best practices and design patterns.
• Work in an agile environment practicing CI/CD principles while participating in operational responsibilities including on-call duties.
• Demonstrate operational excellence through monitoring, troubleshooting, and resolving production issues.
• Design, develop and test software components that interact with fulfillment center technologies, such as robotics, material handling equipment, computer vision, and cloud services.

A day in the life
As an SDE intern/co-op, you will be matched to a manager and a mentor and will have the opportunity to influence the evolution of Amazon technology and lead critical projects early in your career.

In addition to working on an impactful project, you will have the opportunity to engage with Amazonians for both personal and professional development, expand your network, and participate in activities with other interns/co-ops throughout your internship. No matter the location of your internship/co-op, we give you the tools to own your project and learn in a real-world setting.

About the team

• Are 18 years of age or older
• Are enrolled in a Bachelor's degree or above in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, Information Systems, or related STEM fields
• Experience with at least one general-purpose programming language such as Java, Python, C++, C#, Go, Rust, or TypeScript
• Experience with data structure implementation, basic algorithm development, and/or object-oriented design principles

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
• Experience from previous technical internship(s) or demonstrated project experience
• Experience with Cloud platforms (preferably AWS), database systems (SQL and NoSQL), AI tools for development productivity, contributing to open-source projects, and/or version control systems
• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills; Excellent written and verbal communication skills
• Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt to new technologies quickly
• Basic understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC)
• Experience within Robotics, Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Optimization, Manipulation, Perception, Motion Planning and Controls, Movement, Autonomous Navigation, Path Planning, or related

Join Our Team:

As part of our Cinemark Universe, you will immerse yourself in a fun and fast-paced environment that offers rewarding learning experiences. With 500+ theatres and nearly 6,000 screens; we're truly a global presence of 20,000 movie lovers working together to make unforgettable experiences. 

2026 Summer Internship Program: 
Are you ready to launch your career in entertainment and make a real impact? Cinemark’s Corporate Internship Program is your gateway to hands-on experience, professional development, and meaningful connections – all from our dynamic and inclusive corporate office. Join us for the 2026 Summer Internship, taking place June 1st to August 7th at Cinemark’s Service Center in Plano, TX.

What’s in it for you?
• Meaningful Contributions: You’ll work on impactful projects that align with real business goals, giving you the opportunity to make a visible difference. You’ll gain hands-on experience and exposure to the inner workings of a publicly traded company with a global footprint.
• Mentorship & Support: You will be paired with a dedicated peer coach to guide your development and help you navigate the experience.
• Executive Exposure: The program culminates in a final presentation to Cinemark’s executive leadership – your chance to showcase your work and insights to key decision-makers.
• Fun & Engaging Events: Build lasting connections through social activities, development workshops, and exclusive opportunities to engage with senior leaders.
• Full-Time Immersion: A 40-hour workweek during standard business hours gives you a true feel for a professional work environment.
• Hybrid Flexibility: Enjoy a hybrid schedule with 3 days onsite and 2 days remote, designed to balance collaboration and flexibility.
• Competitive Pay: Earn a competitive hourly wage while gaining valuable industry experience and professional development.

The role of a Data Science Intern:

We’re hiring two curious, data‑driven Pricing & Data Science Interns to help unlock insights and shape smarter pricing decisions. You’ll dive into real datasets, contribute to pricing strategy, support operational execution, and sharpen competitive intelligence – partnering with peer coaches and cross‑functional teams across Admission Tickets Pricing, Food & Beverage Pricing, and Competitive Intelligence.

Responsibilities:
• Gather, clean, and preprocess data from multiple sources.
• Analyze trends and correlate performance with market and demographic drivers.
• Assist with scripting/ automation /operation workflow to reduce manual effort and improve reliability.
• Support AI automation via data preparation, prototyping, QA, and data collection
• Create dashboards in Power BI to visualize trends, KPIs, and insights.
• Prepare concise summaries and recommendations for stakeholders.

Requirements:
• Current Junior or Senior pursuing a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Economics, Business, Engineering, or a related field at an accredited university.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; curiosity and willingness to learn.
• Basic knowledge of data analysis tools, including Power BI, Python, SQL, and Excel.
• Exposure to LLMs/ GenAI, prompt engineering, or model evaluation a nice-to-have.
• Strong communication and presentation skills.
• Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
